subject: LU-5092 nodemap: save id maps to targets in new index file

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/26524/

Patch Set 1: Verified-1

Build Failed 
 
https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/26524/ : FAILURE

Patch Set 1:

Andrew, Johann,
Kit is looking for some feedback on the general approach taken by this and the next patch for distributing the nodemap(s) from the MGS to the other targets.  This is intended to be done in a manner similar to the quota tables in 2.4+.

The patches need to be rebased to address build issues with RHEL7, but it would be nice to get some feedback on the overall direction of implementation.

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Patch Set 7:

Kit, the patch is currently marked "fortestonly" which means that it does not get the full set of test runs, and cannot land as-is.  Is this patch finished, or is there more development still needed?

James, Andrew, any chance you could review this patch?  It doesn't look like it has seen much review yet, and even if it isn't 100% completed it would be good to get feedback earlier.

Patch Set 7:

Actually I have this patch in my special sauce which I'm running to test DNE2. Haven't tested it for the node map functionality yet but I have a idea on how to test it besides the sanity-sec.sh test.

Patch Set 7:

I was originally going to hold off on this patch until it saved to OSTs and MDTs, but decided to just deal with that in a later patch, so I'll resubmit this patch without the fortestonly.

Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

(20 comments)

Haven't looked at nodemap_storage.c yet, but comments on the rest of it.

Patch Set 7:

(10 comments)

I think the functionality looks generally correct, but it could be explained more clearly in the comments, since the functions aren't all used and it is hard to see the big picture of how things are done.

Patch Set 7:

(2 comments)

Thanks for the review, I made a couple comments. I'll upload a new patch as well.

Patch Set 22:

I added a new test to the patch that causes a kernel panic on my test machine, due to running out of credits on an index delete. Essentially the test adds 25k new nodemaps and then deletes them. After about 13k delete, the credit issue occurs and the system LBUGs.

Because the credit issue happens after so many iterations, my first thought is that it's an edge case in the index code. I was wondering if that makes sense, and if there were any tips on how to debug an issue like this.

Patch Set 22:

I added some debug code to the IAM code, it looks like iam_txn_dirty can take a credit, iam_index_shrink can take a credit, and iam_recycle_leaf takes a credit. Here's the debug info I got, the credit number is h->h_buffer_credits:
00000001:00000040:1.0:1439827725.734445:0:9062:0:(osd_iam.c:2443:iam_it_rec_delete()) about to delete, credits: 2
00000001:00000040:1.0:1439827725.734447:0:9062:0:(osd_iam.c:2445:iam_it_rec_delete()) about to dirty, credits: 2
00000001:00000040:1.0:1439827725.734449:0:9062:0:(osd_iam.c:2451:iam_it_rec_delete()) about to shrink, credits: 1
00000001:00000040:1.0:1439827725.734453:0:9062:0:(osd_iam.c:2460:iam_it_rec_delete()) about to recycle, credits: 0

iam_recycle_leaf calls iam_txn_dirty, which requires a credit. Without the credit, jbd aborts and Lustre LBUGs. The dump creds function prints this line:
delete: 1/1/2

This seems to indicate that a OSD_OT_DELETE operation should take two credits instead of the one that it's currently taking. I opened LU-7014 to track this issue.

parents.subject: LU-6389 utils: fix lustre_rsync read retry

commit.subject: LU-5092 nodemap: save id maps to targets in new index file
commit.message:
LU-5092 nodemap: save id maps to targets in new index file

Creates a new nodemap index file on registered targets and modifies
it on changes to the nodemaps. On init, the nodemap/idmaps are
loaded into memory from the index file. Currently only the MGS
registers itself.

Signed-off-by: Kit Westneat <kit.westneat@gmail.com>
Change-Id: Ie2b84e25ecc02d5d3daebf268d2f72ffaf142758
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/11813
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
